commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a new `doctor-health-upgrades.md` RFC describing structured Doctor health migration, core/extension ordering, dry-run repair previews, and compatibility notes.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is an RFC-only PR, not a runtime bug report. The review path is source comparison between the proposed RFC text and the referenced merged Doctor implementation.
commands.review_followup: The remaining work is RFC/product-direction review and a source-of-truth correction, not a safe automated repair lane.

target.title: RFC: Doctor health upgrades
target.body:
## Summary

Adds an RFC for the Doctor health upgrade model we are using across the Doctor stack:

- core Doctor contribution order remains the source of truth for built-in checks
- the health registry remains the extension health surface
- rules can migrate in place by adding structured health beside legacy run
- detect/repair and structured run are both supported migration forms
- dry-run repair previews can return findings, diffs, effects, warnings, and changes

## Scope

This is intentionally scoped to Doctor health execution and migration mechanics. It does not define policy semantics or propose public plugin SDK changes.

## Example

The RFC calls out the shell completion migration shape from #85566: structured lint health is declared through the existing Doctor contribution while real repair remains on the legacy Doctor path.
